title: podman struck at starting machine in macos
body: ### Issue Description Just installed podman in macos, after entering command podman machine start podman-machine-default it struck at starting machine "podman-machine-default". I don't see any logs or failures some thing like that. ### Steps to reproduce the issue Steps to reproduce the issue 1. podman machine init 2.podman machine start podman-machine-dafault ### Describe the results you received struck at podman starting ### Describe the results you expected it has to start a vm ### podman info output ```yaml OS: darwin/arm64 provider: qemu version: 4.8.3 Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: failed to connect: ssh: handshake failed: read tcp 127.0.0.1:56467->127.0.0.1:55615: read: connection reset by peer ``` ### Podman in a container No ### Privileged Or Rootless None ### Upstream Latest Release Yes ### Additional environment details Additional environment details ### Additional information Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: If it is Apple Silicon and brew installation, then it is probably a duplicate of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21096 Linked issue has some workarounds listed.
Yeah I have exactly this problem. Can't use podman 😢
Podman still unusable on MacOS after all those weeks. Even the new 4.9.0 on Macos 14.3.

I agree Podman, out of the box, is not starting properly on MacOS. If you follow the detailed workaround instructions in https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21096 you should be able to get it working. At least that worked for me.

Specifically these instructions here worked for me: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21096#issuecomment-1884324640
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Podman 5.0 uses applehv now and qemu is no longer supported.
